title: Intensely Yeasty Thai Chicken Kao Mun Gai
description: A yeasty, creamy, and flavorful Thai dish featuring tahini paste and ferment-kimchi, perfect for a lazy Sunday, vegan-friendly version available by substituting chicken with tofu or tempeh. This dish is a unique twist on traditional Kao Mun Gai.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and saute for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the vegan chicken strips and cook until browned and cooked through.
Add the ferment-kimchi, tahini paste, and coconut milk, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the cooked Jasmine rice and cook for an additional 2 minutes until the rice is well coated with the sauce.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the Kao Mun Gai hot, garnished with additional kimchi and sesame seeds if desired.
